title: TaskTransitioned payload
description: Emitted when a task moves to a non-terminal status — task_start, task_block, task_unblock, task_reopen. Terminal-state transitions have their own events (TaskCompleted, TaskCancelled) because they carry distinct required payload (commit_sha for completed, reason for cancelled). The from/to pair lets readers reconstruct the lifecycle by folding only TaskTransitioned + the terminal-state events; they never need to walk every action. blocker_slug (existing) names the BLOCKER edge ADDED in this transition; removed_blocker_slug (added 2026-05-20 via T3 of agent-substrate-crud-retirement, §9.1) names the BLOCKER edge REMOVED. Per the §9.1 audit finding, T3 also lifts the L1181 guard so 2nd+ blocker INSERTs emit a (blocked → blocked) self-transition with blocker_slug=<new>; this lets payload-only fold reconstruct proj_task_blockers without joining the CRUD table.

chain_slug.description: Chain slug disambiguating which chain's task this event targets, so the fold scopes to one task rather than fanning out across same-slug tasks in the project (bug task-lifecycle-event-folds-fan-out-across-duplicate-task-slugs). Optional: pre-fix events omit it and the fold falls back to the legacy slug+project match.

from_status.description: Status before the transition. All five values are reachable as from_status because task_reopen sources from closed/cancelled, task_unblock from blocked, task_start from pending, etc.

to_status.description: Status after the transition. Restricted to the three non-terminal values — closed and cancelled emit TaskCompleted / TaskCancelled instead.

blocker_slug.description: For to_status="blocked" with a specific blocker pinned via task_block(blocker_slug=...): the slug of the blocking task being ADDED. Optional — task_block also accepts being called without a blocker (just marks status=blocked) and in that case this field is null. For non-blocked transitions, always null. Post-T3 (§9.1 guard lift): also populated for the blocked→blocked self-transition case when a 2nd+ blocker is added to an already-blocked task.

removed_blocker_slug.description: Added 2026-05-20 via T3 of agent-substrate-crud-retirement (§9.1 audit finding). For unblock transitions: the slug of the blocker edge being REMOVED. Multi-edge unblock-all emits one TaskTransitioned per removed edge (each with this field populated); single-edge unblock emits one event with this field populated. Null for blocker-adding and non-blocker-edge transitions. Payload-only fold reconstruction reads this to drop the right proj_task_blockers row.
